title: Forge conflict adjudication (.forge/adjudication.json)
description: Per-active-root project state: the project's CONFLICT-adjudication policy and its recorded human choices. A CONFLICT is exactly a read-view uid with >= 2 distinct candidate records — i.e. a dedup uid-collision or near-dup across the project's library-local + subscribed-slice sources (ADR-0017 §5.1, ADR-0018 read-view). Conflicts are DERIVED deterministically from the catalog records (manager/catalog.mjs) and are never stored; only the POLICY and the human CHOICES live here. The adjudication POLICY is per-criticality (safety|compliance|normal, ADR-0013) and is either "auto" or "block"; the DEFAULT is all-block (conservative — every conflict needs an explicit human pick). A recorded CHOICE is a human T2 decision (ADR-0017 §7 / BR-CAT-003) naming the winning sourceId (or null for the library-local copy) for a conflict uid. The CLI is DETERMINISTIC-COLLECTION only (METHOD §7): it COLLECTS conflicts and CONSUMES any recorded judge verdict (.forge/catalog-verdicts.json, BR-CAT-001) + eval scores, but it NEVER invokes the judge agent or any model, and never produces a verdict. Resolving a conflict with --apply updates the composition (.forge/composition.json) via manager/compose.mjs so the winner's (uid, sourceId) is adopted and the losing peers for that uid are removed; this is a T2 human action and never a self-applied replace of an admitted library resource (BR-CAT-003), even under policy "auto". Owned by manager/conflict.mjs (forge conflict list|resolve|policy). Reads/writes go through manager/lib/store.mjs (readJson / writeJsonAtomic); writes are ADDITIVE and never destructive (resolve sets/clears one choice; policy sets one or more dimensions), preview by default and written only on --apply (the file is created on first --apply). This file lives UNDER the active project root, not in the git-tracked library.

schema.description: On-disk schema tag for this file.

_note.description: Free-form authoring note; ignored by the conflict operator.

choices.description: The recorded human adjudications. Each entry names the WINNER for a conflict uid (the human T2 pick, ADR-0017 §7 / BR-CAT-003). An absent uid is UNRESOLVED. Order is not significant; entries SHOULD be unique on uid (the operator dedups and keeps the last write per uid). A choice never auto-applies a replace of an already-admitted library resource — that remains a deliberate human --apply (BR-CAT-003).

policy.description: The per-criticality adjudication policy. Each criticality (safety|compliance|normal, ADR-0013) maps to "auto" (a conflict with a graceful suggested winner may resolve without a per-conflict human pick) or "block" (the conflict is BLOCKING until a human resolves it). The DEFAULT is all-block — the conservative stance consistent with sources defaulting untrusted (ADR-0017) and slices defaulting unsubscribed (ADR-0018). NOTE: even under "auto", a resolve that would REPLACE an already-admitted library resource is a T2 human action (BR-CAT-003) and is never self-applied — "auto" governs only composition-level (per-project adoption) picks, never a library replace.

policyMode.description: How conflicts of a given criticality are adjudicated: "auto" (resolvable without a per-conflict human pick when a suggested winner exists) or "block" (BLOCKING until an explicit human resolve). Default "block".

choice.description: One recorded human adjudication: the winning candidate for a conflict uid. winner is the sourceId of the chosen candidate, or null for the library-local copy (source === null). Recording a choice flips the conflict's state to "manual"; on --apply the winner's (uid, sourceId) is adopted into the composition and the losing peers for that uid are removed (manager/compose.mjs).

uid.description: A resource uid "<kind>:<id>": the singular registry kind (agent|skill|command|rule|hook|bundle|validator|mcp|meta-test|engine) and the resource id, joined by a single colon (ADR-0005 / the registry record). This is the conflict key — the uid that resolves to >= 2 distinct candidate records in the read-view.

winner.description: The chosen candidate for this conflict: the source id (manifests/sources.json#sources[].id) of the winning candidate, or null for the library-local copy (source === null, ADR-0017).

oneOf.description: A source id (manifests/sources.json#sources[].id) — the winning candidate came from this source.
The library-local copy (source === null) is the winner.
